name: Crucifier
short_description: Crucifier is the worlds most Christian racing game ever developed! Play as a Roman soldier as he completes his day! Compete in 3 races to get on the top score boards! Farm your local fields for crops to obtain enough money to rest comfortably! Talk to lovely locals and battle a crazy time traveler!
detailed_description:
Crucifier is the worlds most Christian racing game!

Play as a Roman soldier in historical time as you compete in three exhilarating races! Try not only to beat your fellow Roman soldiers in your quest to come in first but also compete to get on the leaderboards with your best time!

But wait! There's more!!! In between races you can farm the local fields to obtain enough money before you peacefully slumber at night! Talk with encouraging locals that will give you advice that we all need in life! You'll not only meet wonderful middle eastern locals, but you'll encounter a mysterious time traveler hellbent on stopping you from competing against yourself in the final race!

Try and try again as you best your top scores in each run of the game!
about_the_game:

Play as a Roman soldier in historical time as you compete in three exhilarating races! Try not only to beat your fellow Roman soldiers in your quest to come in first but also compete to get on the leaderboards with your best time!

But wait! There's more!!! In between races you can farm the local fields to obtain enough money before you peacefully slumber at night! You'll not only meet wonderful middle eastern locals, but you'll encounter a mysterious time traveler hellbent on stopping you from competing against yourself in the final race!

reviews.review_text: Crucifier is easily one of the most offensive games I have ever played, and the only reason I played it at all was because I got it through Curator Connect for free from the developer and wanted to experience it firsthand. I could see this being a short game on Newgrounds from 2006 (which could be a compliment to the developer), but not something I would realistically pay anything for. I can sense the inspiration from South Park in the walk cycles, blatant shock value tied to religious themes, and Kurt Russell, if that means anything. I give it a single point for the developer committing to the ridiculous premise, but otherwise, huge pass.
